page: Amazon Web Services m8g.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Utilizing the arm64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with one thread per core. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance, with the Passmark memory score placing in the top 10% tier. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks show strong results in the top 25% tier, reaching 296.67 GB/sec and 133.88 GB/sec respectively. Computational performance is also robust, with Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores falling in the top 25% tier. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Passmark database operations also rank in the top 25% tier. However, LLM inference tasks present a mixed profile; while small model inference remains average, prompt processing for medium-sized 7B models falls into the bottom 25% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8g.4xlarge offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, leveraging the arm64 architecture to deliver high memory bandwidth and solid database operation throughput without the premium cost of x86 architectures. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 8 Gbps. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded decompression tasks, database operations, and text processing applications, though it is less optimal for intensive medium-to-large LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.4xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show top-tier memory performance, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10% and strong uncached memory read and write bandwidth in the top 25%. Computational benchmarks, including Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, also rank in the top 25%, alongside strong database operations and multi-threaded decompression. However, prompt processing for medium LLM models is a relative bottleneck, placing in the bottom 25%. This instance is suited for database operations, text processing, and general-purpose applications requiring high memory bandwidth.
